item.title: Saudi Oil Exports Value Surged to Three-Year High in March
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s revenue from oil exports jumped to a more than three-year high of $24.7 billion in the first full month of the war in the Middle East, as the kingdom’s ability to divert shipments through the Red Sea coupled with higher energy prices compensated for disruptions from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: French Business Activity Slumps at Fastest Pace Since 2020
item.description: French business activity shrank at the quickest pace in five and a half years as higher energy prices hit consumers and firms.

item.title: Sri Lankan Rupee Weakens to Three-Year Low as Oil Gains Weigh
item.description: The Sri Lankan rupee weakened to a three-year low on Thursday, underperforming all Asian peers, as a gain in oil prices weighed on the currency.

item.title: South Africa Gains Lift Investec Profit, Dividend to Record
item.description: Investec posted a record dividend for the fourth straight year as the South Africa- and UK-listed bank’s full-year profit climbed to a new high.

item.title: BT Sees Revenue Declines as UK Competition Rises
item.description: BT Group Plc forecast declining revenues for 2027 that missed analyst estimates, as Chief Executive Officer Allison Kirkby continues a turnaround plan to stem customer losses and fend off growing competition.

item.title: AMD Commits More Than $10 Billion to Taiwan AI Investments
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices Inc., the leading challenger to Nvidia Corp. in AI computing chips, pledged to invest more than $10 billion in Taiwan to expand partnerships and add packaging capacity.

item.title: South Korea Set to Reduce Debt Sales in June as Bonds Slump
item.description: South Korea will cut its debt issuance in June, the finance ministry said, as the government steps up its defense of the bond market.

item.title: Stock Rally Takes a Breather on Rebound in Oil Prices
UK and Gulf States Hail Trade Deal After Four Years of Talks
item.description: The UK signed a long-awaited trade deal with the Gulf Cooperation Council on Thursday, deepening its ties with the oil-rich nations at a time of rising geopolitical tension and economic pressures.

item.title: India’s Economic Activity Holds Steady in May, Flash PMIs Show
item.description: India’s economic activity remained stable in May from a month ago, a flash survey by HSBC Holdings Plc showed Thursday.

item.title: Circular AI Boom Goes Global as Asia Windfall Funds Hyperscalers
item.description: The windfall earned by Asian chip makers is coursing through the world economy, mirroring on a global scale the circular flow of money within the AI ecosystem.

item.title: Norway’s Europe-Leading Stock Rally Goes Beyond Oil Heavyweights
item.description: Norway is developed Europe’s best-performing stock market this year, thanks to surging energy prices. But there’s more attracting investors to Oslo than just oil and gas.

item.title: Oil Near $100 Emerges as Consensus for Next Year With Iran War
item.description: Oil market participants are increasingly pricing crude to be capped near $100 a barrel over the next year, as demand is forced to slow down to counter millions of barrels of supply losses caused by the US-Iran war.

item.title: Nvidia’s Huang Spurs Asia Tech Rally With AI, Robots Hype
item.description: The latest report from Nvidia Corp. may have left its investors wanting more but it gave a jolt to the shares of a broad swath of Asian tech companies.

item.title: Seoul Apartment Price Rally Gains Pace Ahead of BOK Decision
item.description: The housing rally in South Korea’s capital continues to regain momentum, an outcome that is likely to reinforce concerns at the nation’s central bank that rising property prices could complicate the policy outlook as the Iran conflict fuels inflationary pressures.

item.title: Bank Indonesia Seen Hiking Again by Analysts as Pressure Mounts
item.description: Indonesia’s central bank is set to raise interest rates further to bolster the rupiah, but this and other policy steps may fail to prevent further losses in the nation’s assets, analysts say.

item.title: Dimon Says Rates Could Go Much Higher Even After Bond Selloff
item.description: Jamie Dimon said interest rates may climb much higher from current levels, a warning to bond investors at a time when yields have touched multi-year highs.

item.title: Citi Sees India IPOs Hitting Fresh Records Despite Challenges
item.description: Initial public offerings in India should get back on track in the second half of the year, according to Citigroup Inc., moving on from a rocky start that’s seen foreign investors withdraw capital and the rupee slide.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Turns Nvidia Into a sNoozefest
item.description: What’s more exciting than 85% sales growth? A colony on Mars.

item.title: Base Metals Drift as US and Iran Trade Threats on Peace Deal
item.description: Copper wavered between narrow gains and losses after President Donald Trump claimed the US was in the final stages of talks with Iran, even as the two nations continued to trade threats of fresh escalation.

item.title: Indian Rupee Rises Most in Asia on Central Bank Intervention
item.description: India’s rupee advanced the most in Asia after the central bank intervened and as policymakers are said to be considering options, including an interest-rate hike, to defend the currency.

item.title: JSW Motors Gets $826 Million Funding From India’s Top Bank
item.description: Billionaire Sajjan Jindal’s automotive business has secured about 80 billion rupees ($826 million) funding line from India’s largest lender, keeping its plans for a new energy vehicle venture on track.

item.title: Iran War Spurs Extreme Bear Scenarios for Asia Currencies, Bonds
item.description: The Iran war is piling pressure on emerging Asian markets, pushing some currencies and bond yields toward levels once considered unlikely.

item.title: Dimon Says JPMorgan Will Hire More for AI, Fewer Bankers
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Chief Executive Officer Jamie Dimon said the Wall Street giant will likely hire more artificial intelligence specialists and fewer traditional bankers as the adoption of the technology accelerates.

item.title: India Weighs Measures Including Rate Hike to Stabilize Rupee
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India is considering all of its available options to stabilize the rupee, including an interest rate hike, more currency swaps and raising dollars from investors overseas, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Why Australians Are Fed Up With Their Biggest Supermarkets
item.description: A court ruling against Coles is fueling fresh anger over grocery prices and supermarket power.

item.title: Ambani’s $4 Billion Jio IPO Hits Roadblocks on Iran War Impact
item.description: Reliance Industries Ltd.’s plan to sell shares in digital arm Jio Platforms Ltd. — potentially India’s biggest-ever initial public offering — is running into a thicket of challenges exacerbated by the war in Iran.

item.title: Goldman Says Global Oil Stockpiles Falling at Record Pace on War
item.description: Global stockpiles of crude oil and products are being drawn down at a record pace this month as the war in the Middle East drags on, curtailing supplies, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Global Funds Brace for More Rupee Losses With 100 in Sight
item.description: Record foreign outflows from local stocks keep currency under pressure.

item.title: Samsung AI Bonus Spat Tests South Korea’s Labor-Friendly Leader
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung came to power promising stronger labor protection and social cohesion, while vowing to transform South Korea into an AI powerhouse competing with the US and China. Now, those ambitions are starting to collide.
